briefdescription.para: Channel ready for upper layer traffic on it.

detaileddescription.para: Used only by internal APIs dealing with setting channel to proper state depending on operational context.

briefdescription.para: The ISO packet may possibly contain errors.

detaileddescription.para: May be caused by a failed CRC check or if missing a part of the SDU.
If not set, then the bt_iso_recv_info.ts value is not valid, and should not be used.
Register ISO server, each new connection is authorized using the accept() callback which in case of success shall allocate the channel structure to be used by the new connection.

parameterdescription.para: Server structure.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

detaileddescription.para: Unregister previously registered ISO server.

parameterdescription.para: Server structure.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Creates a CIG as a central.

detaileddescription.para: This can called at any time, even before connecting to a remote device. This must be called before any connected isochronous stream (CIS) channel can be connected.
Once a CIG is created, the channels supplied in the param can be connected using bt_iso_chan_connect().

parameterdescription.para: The parameters used to create and enable the CIG.
Connected Isochronous Group object on success.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Reconfigure a CIG as a central.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used to update a CIG. It will update the group specific parameters, and, if supplied, change the QoS parameters of the individual CIS. If the cis_channels in param contains CIS that was not originally in the call to bt_iso_cig_create(), these will be added to the group. It is not possible to remove any CIS from the group after creation.
This can be called at any time before connecting an ISO to a remote device. Once any CIS in the group has connected, the group cannot be changed.
Once a CIG is created, the channels supplied in the param can be connected using bt_iso_chan_connect().

parameterdescription.para: Connected Isochronous Group object.
The parameters used to reconfigure the CIG.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Terminates a CIG as a central.

detaileddescription.para: All the CIS in the CIG shall be disconnected first.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the CIG structure.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Connect ISO channels on ACL connections.

detaileddescription.para: Connect ISO channels. The ISO channels must have been initialized in a CIG first by calling bt_iso_cig_create().
Once the connection is completed the channels' connected() callback will be called. If the connection is rejected disconnected() callback is called instead.
This function will also setup the ISO data path based on the path parameter of the bt_iso_chan_io_qos for each channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a connect parameter array with the ISO and ACL pointers.
Number of connect parameters.
Successfully started the connecting procedure.
Invalid parameters were supplied.

Some ISO channels are already being connected. It is not possible to have multiple outstanding connection requests. May also be returned if CONFIG_BT_SMP is enabled and a pairing procedure is already in progress.
Not buffers available to send request to controller or if CONFIG_BT_SMP is enabled and no more keys could be stored.
If CONFIG_BT_SMP is enabled and no more keys could be stored.
Controller rejected the request or if CONFIG_BT_SMP is enabled and pairing has timed out.
If CONFIG_BT_SMP is enabled the ACL is not connected.

detaileddescription.para: Disconnect connected ISO channel.
If the device is a central and the connection is pending it will be canceled and as a result the channel bt_iso_chan_ops.disconnected() callback is called.

If the device is a peripheral and the connection is pending it will be rejected, as a peripheral shall wait for a CIS Established event (which may trigger a bt_iso_chan_ops.disconnected() callback in case of an error).
Regarding to input parameter, to get details see reference description to bt_iso_chan_connect() API.

parameterdescription.para: Channel object.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Send data to ISO channel without timestamp.

detaileddescription.para: Send data from buffer to the channel. If credits are not available, buf will be queued and sent as and when credits are received from peer. Regarding to first input parameter, to get details see reference description to bt_iso_chan_connect() API.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Channel object.
Buffer containing data to be sent.
Packet Sequence number. This value shall be incremented for each call to this function and at least once per SDU interval for a specific channel.

simplesect.para: Number of octets sent in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_iso_chan *chan, struct net_buf *buf, uint16_t seq_num, uint32_t ts)
memberdef.name: bt_iso_chan_send_ts

briefdescription.para: Send data to ISO channel with timestamp.

detaileddescription.para: Send data from buffer to the channel. If credits are not available, buf will be queued and sent as and when credits are received from peer. Regarding to first input parameter, to get details see reference description to bt_iso_chan_connect() API.

simplesect.para: Buffer ownership is transferred to the stack in case of success, in case of an error the caller retains the ownership of the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Channel object.
Buffer containing data to be sent.
Packet Sequence number. This value shall be incremented for each call to this function and at least once per SDU interval for a specific channel.
Timestamp of the SDU in microseconds (us). This value can be used to transmit multiple SDUs in the same SDU interval in a CIG or BIG.

simplesect.para: Number of octets sent in case of success or negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_iso_chan *chan, uint8_t dir, const struct bt_iso_chan_path *path)
memberdef.name: bt_iso_setup_data_path

briefdescription.para: Sets up the ISO data path for a ISO channel.

detaileddescription.para: The channel must be associated with a BIS or CIS handle first which it is when the bt_iso_chan_ops.connected() callback is called.

parameterdescription.para: The channel to setup the ISO data path for

The direction to setup, either BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_CTLR_TO_HOST or BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_HOST_TO_CTLR. For ISO broadcast channels this can only be BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_HOST_TO_CTLR, and for ISO sync receiver channels this can only be BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_CTLR_TO_HOST.
The data path
Success
Invalid parameters
No HCI command buffer could be allocated
The controller rejected the request or response contains invalid data
chan is not associated with a CIS or BIS handle
The controller rejected the request as disallowed
Unexpected error occurred

briefdescription.para: Removes the ISO data path for a ISO channel.

detaileddescription.para: Removes the ISO data path configured by bt_iso_setup_data_path() for the provided dir.

The data paths of CIS for Peripherals are deleted by the controller, and thus it is not necessary (or possible) to remove data paths of CIS after they have disconnected for a Peripheral, as per Bluetooth Core specification 6.0, Vol 4, Part E, Section 7.7.5. The data paths for CIS for a Central remain valid, even after a disconnection, and thus a Central device should call bt_iso_remove_data_path() on disconnect if it no longer wants to use that CIS. All data paths created by a Central are removed when the CIG is removed with bt_iso_cig_terminate().

Any data paths associated with an ISO Sync Receiver BIG are removed by the controller when the BIG sync is lost or terminated, and thus it is not necessary (or possible) to remove data paths of ISO channels associated with a BIG for a Sync Receiver, as per Bluetooth Core specification 6.0, Vol 4, Part E, Section 7.7.65.30

All data paths associated with an ISO Broadcaster BIG are removed when the BIG is terminated by bt_iso_big_terminate(), and thus it is not necessary (or possible) to remove data paths of ISO channels associated with a BIG for a Broadcaster, as per Bluetooth Core specification 6.0, Vol 4, Part E, Section 7.8.105

parameterdescription.para: The channel to setup the ISO data path for

The direction to setup, either BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_CTLR_TO_HOST or BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_HOST_TO_CTLR. For ISO broadcast channels this can only be BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_HOST_TO_CTLR, and for ISO sync receiver channels this can only be BT_HCI_DATAPATH_DIR_CTLR_TO_HOST.
Success
Invalid parameters
No HCI command buffer could be allocated
The controller rejected the request or response contains invalid data
chan is not associated with a CIS or BIS handle
The controller rejected the request as disallowed
Unexpected error occurred
Channel object.
Channel info object.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

detaileddescription.para: Reads timing information for transmitted ISO packet on an ISO channel. The HCI_LE_Read_ISO_TX_Sync HCI command is used to retrieve this information from the controller.

simplesect.para: An SDU must have already been successfully transmitted on the ISO channel for this function to return successfully.

parameterdescription.para: Channel object.
Transmit info object.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code on failure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the callback structure.
on success
if cb is NULL
if cb is already registered

briefdescription.para: Creates a BIG as a broadcaster.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the periodic advertising object the BIGInfo shall be sent on.
The parameters used to create and enable the BIG. The QOS parameters are determined by the QOS field of the first BIS in the BIS list of this parameter.
Broadcast Isochronous Group object on success.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Terminates a BIG as a broadcaster or receiver.

detaileddescription.para: This function cannot be called while in bt_iso_big_cb::started, bt_iso_big_cb::stopped, bt_iso_chan_ops::connected or bt_iso_chan_ops::disconnected callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the BIG structure.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Creates a BIG as a receiver.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the periodic advertising sync object the BIGInfo was received on.
The parameters used to create and enable the BIG sync.
Broadcast Isochronous Group object on success.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Headroom needed for outgoing ISO SDUs.
Helper to calculate needed buffer size for ISO SDUs.

detaileddescription.para: Useful for creating buffer pools.

parameterdescription.para: Required ISO SDU size

simplesect.para: Needed buffer size to match the requested ISO SDU MTU.

detaileddescription.para: The BIS indexes start from 0x01, so the lowest allowed bit is BIT(0) that represents index 0x01. To synchronize to e.g. BIS indexes 0x01 and 0x02, the bitfield value should be BIT(0) | BIT(1). As a general notation, to sync to BIS index N use BIT(N - 1).

briefdescription.para: Value to set the ISO data path over HCi.
The minimum value for vendor specific data path ID.
The maximum value for vendor specific data path ID.
Minimum controller delay in microseconds (0 us)
Maximum controller delay in microseconds (4,000,000 us)
Minimum interval value in microseconds (255 us)
Maximum interval value in microseconds (1,048,575 us)
Minimum ISO interval in units of 1.25 ms (5 ms)
Maximum ISO interval in units of 1.25 ms (4,000 ms)
Minimum latency value in milliseconds (5 ms)
Maximum latency value in milliseconds (4,000 ms)
Packets will be sent sequentially between the channels in the group.
Packets will be sent interleaved between the channels in the group.
Packets may be framed or unframed.
Maximum number of isochronous channels in a single group (31)
Minimum flush timeout in multiples of ISO interval (1)
Maximum flush timeout in multiples of ISO interval (255)
Minimum BIG sync timeout value in units of 10 ms (100 ms)
Maximum BIG sync timeout value in units of 10 ms (163,840 ms)
Controller controlled maximum subevent count value.
Minimum BIG sync maximum subevent count value (1)
Maximum BIG sync maximum subevent count value (31)

detaileddescription.para: This maximum depends on the ISO interval, as the subinterval shall be less than the ISO interval

briefdescription.para: Check if ISO BIS bitfield is valid (BT_ISO_BIS_INDEX_BIT(1)|..|BT_ISO_BIS_INDEX_BIT(31))

parameterdescription.para: BIS index bitfield (uint32)
